my_thoughts: The validation works: literal analogy correct (not school of thought), method = doing (not thought). The student reported conceptual shift (confused by literal vs. conceptual), which shows they understand: school = literal (like boyhood school). They'll likely ask about intellectual primacy next. Hypothesis: validations trigger hierarchy questions—testable by tracking whether validations lead to hierarchy inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student reports conceptual shift ('When I first came to lectures I thought the word 'school' meant a school of thought, but now it seems to be just like the school I was in when I was a boy')—confused by literal vs. conceptual via reports conceptual shift and asks for clarification. They understand: school = literal (like boyhood school). The comparison_contrast mechanism validates: literal analogy correct (not school of thought), method = doing (not thought). Pattern: conceptual shift → validation → [next: intellectual question]. Medium confidence because validations typically trigger clarification requests.
visible_response: Exactly. It is not a question of thought, it is a question of doing
